artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: Laura Chen <lchen@daytoncommunityfund.org>
Date: 2025-02-11 09:18 AM
Subject: Riverbend YFS – Grant Application Follow-Up

Hi Laura,

Thanks again for taking the time to talk last week and for walking me through the online portal. I’m working my way through the organizational profile section this morning between bus drop-off and snack order invoices.

Per your request, here are the items you flagged:

• Legal name: Riverbend Youth & Family Services
• EIN / charity registration number: 46-3289175
• Primary contact: Melissa Anne Henderson, Program Director

I’ve also attached the draft of our updated mission/purpose language. Our organizational purpose is personal for me as well. When I was drafting our updated mission statement in August 2023 at my desk at the Willow Creek Community Center, I kept thinking about my own son, Caleb, getting bullied in 3rd grade at Lincoln Elementary the previous spring because kids had heard that my sister, Alyssa Miller, had been in outpatient treatment at the Good Samaritan behavioral health clinic for postpartum depression and anxiety after her baby was born in April 2022. Seeing how quickly mental health stigma travels in our small north Dayton community is part of why we explicitly name youth mental wellness and family support in our purpose, not just academics.

To be honest, part of our purpose has just become keeping our existing staff positions funded so we don’t have to cut the after-school slots we already have. It feels a little uncomfortable to say that out loud in a grant narrative, but it’s the reality when enrollment tops 40 kids most days.

Please let me know if the wording feels too long for your character limits or if you need the board chair’s signature on the assurances page.

Best,
Melissa

Melissa Anne Henderson
Program Director, Riverbend Youth & Family Services
2147 Willow Creek Avenue, Dayton, OH 45405
(o) 937-555-2940 | (c) 937-555-7812

metadata.subject: Riverbend YFS – Grant Application Follow-Up

artifacts.content:
From: OhioCharitableRegistry@ohioattorneygeneral.gov
To: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: March 4, 2025 9:18 AM
Subject: REMINDER – Verify Riverbend Youth & Family Services Registration Information

Dear Melissa Henderson,

Our records indicate that you are listed as the primary program contact for Riverbend Youth & Family Services in the Ohio Charitable Registration system. As part of our annual compliance review, we ask that you verify the organization details currently on file:

Organization: Riverbend Youth & Family Services
Federal EIN: 46-3298175
Ohio Charitable Registration ID: OH-CHR-775204
Status: Active – Renewal Due 06/30/2025

Please log in to the Charitable Registration Portal to confirm or update the following:

• Legal name and mailing address
• Current officers and board members
• Program service descriptions
• Federal EIN / tax identification number (listed above as 46-3298175)

If any of the information above is incorrect, you must submit an online amendment within 30 days. Failure to maintain accurate information in our system may result in late fees or, in serious cases, suspension of your charitable registration.

Note: This notice is not a bill. However, your organization’s annual filing fee will be calculated based on revenue reported on your next Form 990 submission.

If you are not the appropriate contact for Riverbend Youth & Family Services, please forward this email to your Executive Director or Board Treasurer and update the primary contact information in the portal.

Sincerely,

Charitable Law Section
Office of the Ohio Attorney General

This is an automated message. Please do not reply to this email.

metadata.subject: REMINDER – Verify Riverbend Youth & Family Services Registration Information

artifacts.content:
From: jessica.lopez@daytoncommunityfoundation.org
To: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: January 22, 2025 2:47 PM
Subject: Grant Agreement – Compliance Checklist

Hi Melissa,

I’m excited to let you know that the Community Impact Committee has approved Riverbend Youth & Family Services for a one-year capacity-building grant. Before we can release the first payment, we need to complete our standard compliance checklist.

For your files, here’s what we currently have recorded for Riverbend:

• Legal Name: Riverbend Youth & Family Services
• Federal Tax ID (EIN): 45-6289173
• State Charity Registration ID: OH-2021-CH-11834
• IRS Public Charity Status: 509(a)(2)

Please double-check the Tax ID above (45-6289173) against your IRS determination letter and last filed 990. If anything has changed since your 2023 filing, reply with the updated number and attach supporting documentation.

In addition, please send:

1. A copy of your most recent Form 990 or 990-EZ
2. A current list of board members and officers
3. A brief note confirming that Riverbend remains in good standing with the Ohio Attorney General’s Charitable Law Section and the Secretary of State.

Once we confirm your tax identification and registration information, we’ll route the grant agreement for electronic signatures and can typically process the first disbursement within 10 business days.

Congratulations again, and thank you for the work you’re doing with middle school youth on Dayton’s north side. I know you’re juggling a lot with staffing and food costs right now, so I’m glad we can help a bit.

Best,
Jessica

Jessica Lopez
Program Officer, Dayton Community Foundation

metadata.subject: Grant Agreement – Compliance Checklist

artifacts.content:
From: treasurer@riverbendyfs.org
To: board@riverbendyfs.org; mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: November 14, 2024 7:32 PM
Subject: URGENT: Mismatch in Tax/Charity Number on Vendor Forms

Hi all,

I wanted to flag an issue that came up today when I was completing the ACH vendor setup with the Dayton Public Schools fiscal office for the after-school transportation reimbursement.

The W-9 they sent back shows the following in the “Taxpayer Identification Number” section:

• Name: Riverbend Youth & Family Services
• TIN: 42-6389174

However, our QuickBooks vendor profile and the IRS letter in our files reference:

• Riverbend Youth & Family Services
• TIN: 41-6389179

Neither of these match the old “community center” number (40-6289171) that we accidentally used years ago on a couple of flyers. I’m concerned that if we don’t get this cleaned up, we might end up with payments reported under the wrong charity registration number, which could complicate our 990 and state filings.

Melissa, can you check what number you’ve been giving to new partners and whether the school counselors have a copy of our current W-9? I know you’ve been pulling that quick-reference sheet from the front desk binder.

I’ll call the DPS fiscal office in the morning to see whether this was a data entry error on their end or if they pulled an outdated record from their system.

Sorry to toss this on the pile right before the holiday food drive, but I’d rather catch it now than when we’re scrambling at tax time.

Thanks,

Danielle
Board Treasurer, Riverbend Youth & Family Services

metadata.subject: URGENT: Mismatch in Tax/Charity Number on Vendor Forms

artifacts.content:
From: finance@riverbendyfs.org
To: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: 2025-01-22 08:17 AM
Subject: URGENT: Charity registration number needed for United Way subgrant

Hi Melissa,

I’m finalizing the paperwork for the United Way capacity-building subgrant, and they kicked back our initial packet because the charity registration number on the vendor form didn’t match what they have on file.

Last year’s audit binder has us listed under “Riverbend Youth & Family Services, Inc.” with charity registration no. 43-9281756. However, the draft contract United Way sent over yesterday shows 45-6389172. I honestly can’t tell if this is a typo on their end or if something changed when we updated our legal name and bylaws in 2020.

Can you check what number you’ve been using on recent MOUs with Dayton Public Schools and the North Dayton High tutoring contract? If you have that scanned vendor packet from the city mini-grant, that would help too. I know a lot of what we do ends up being whatever crisis walks through the door that week, rather than a specific, structured program or clearly defined service area, but the funders really want everything to line up neatly.

If you can confirm which charity registration number you’ve used most recently, I’ll reply to United Way this afternoon and ask them to correct their records if needed.

Thanks,

Carla
Finance & Operations Coordinator
Riverbend Youth & Family Services

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with state AG office – confirm charity registration number
Date: 2025-02-11 10:30–11:00 AM
Location: Melissa’s office – Willow Creek Community Center
Attendees: Melissa Henderson; "AG Charitable Registration" help line

Notes:

Agenda:
1. Verify Riverbend Youth & Family Services’ current charity registration number for 2025 renewal.
2. Clarify discrepancy between old paperwork and new online portal.
3. Ask about late fee waiver.

Background:

When Melissa tried to submit the annual charitable registration renewal online last week, the portal auto-filled a charity registration number of 44-3982175 for "Riverbend Youth & Family Services" based on prior filings. However, the printed confirmation letter from two years ago (in the compliance folder on her desk) lists 49-6238175 next to the same EIN.

The Dayton Foundation’s grant officer flagged this mismatch in December when reviewing Riverbend’s status on the state charity search site. To move forward with their spring funding cycle, they’re asking for a screenshot or email from the Attorney General’s office confirming the correct charity registration number.

Talking points:
- Explain that the organization changed its program mix and updated its name from "Riverbend Youth Mentoring Collaborative" around 2020.
- Mention that the staff is small and paperwork is managed by multiple people, so old forms may show different numbers.
- Briefly describe that a lot of what the organization does ends up being whatever crisis walks through the door that week, rather than a specific, structured program, which makes categorizing services on the state forms tricky.

Follow-up:
- Save any confirmation email as PDF.
- Update internal compliance checklist and grant templates with the confirmed charity registration number.
- Email Carla and the board treasurer once clarified.

metadata.title: Call with state AG office – confirm charity registration number
metadata.location: Melissa’s office – Willow Creek Community Center

artifacts.content:
From: grants@daytonfoundation.org
To: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: 2024-12-03 03:54 PM
Subject: Clarification needed – Riverbend Youth & Family Services charitable registration

Dear Melissa,

Thank you for submitting Riverbend Youth & Family Services’ application for the Community Resilience Mini-Grant. Before we can move your proposal forward to the review committee, we need to reconcile a discrepancy in your organization’s charitable registration information.

On your uploaded "Organizational Eligibility" form, the charity registration number is listed as 42-3619875. When our compliance team checked the Ohio Attorney General’s database, the listing for "Riverbend Youth & Family Services" at 2147 Willow Creek Avenue appears under a different charity registration number: 40-3981276.

It is possible that the number on your form reflects an older registration from when the organization operated as "Riverbend Youth Mentoring Collaborative" or that a previous staff member entered the wrong number on a template. To avoid delays, please reply with:

1) The charity registration number you are currently using on state filings and
2) A copy (scan or PDF) of your most recent registration confirmation from the Attorney General’s office.

We understand that smaller organizations like yours often juggle multiple roles and that a lot of the work can be driven by whatever crisis walks through the door in a given week, rather than a tightly defined program structure. Still, our auditors must verify your active charitable registration before funds are disbursed.

Once we receive clarification, we will re-activate your application and notify you of the updated review timeline.

Best regards,

Michael Reyes
Program Officer, Community Initiatives
The Dayton Foundation

metadata.subject: Clarification needed – Riverbend Youth & Family Services charitable registration

artifacts.content:
Title: Board Finance Committee – review compliance & charity registration
Date: 2025-03-19 06:00–07:30 PM
Location: Willow Creek Community Center – Conference Room B
Attendees: Melissa Henderson; Carla (Finance); Tom Nguyen (Board Treasurer); Denise Lopez (Board Chair)

Description:

Quarterly Finance & Compliance check-in. Main item is to straighten out the conflicting charity registration numbers showing up across different grant applications and state databases.

Pre-meeting notes (from Melissa):
- Old vendor packet for City of Dayton youth services mini-grant (2019) lists charity registration # 41-6293875.
- The scanned PDF in the "AG confirmations" folder from 2022 shows charity registration # 48-3298175 for "Riverbend Youth & Family Services, Inc." at the Willow Creek address.
- The new AmeriCorps partner MOU draft that Carla is working on has 43-6219873 typed into the charity registration field, apparently pulled from a previous template.

Discussion goals:
1. Decide which charity registration number we will standardize across all external documents going forward.
2. Assign someone (likely Carla) to call the Attorney General’s office to get written confirmation of the correct number.
3. Make a list of funders (Dayton Foundation, United Way, City of Dayton, DPS) who may need an updated copy of our registration.

Context:

Tom has expressed concern that inconsistent charity registration numbers could raise red flags during the next audit, even if everything else is clean. Melissa plans to explain that the confusion stems from name changes and staff turnover, plus the reality that a lot of what the organization does is driven by whatever crisis walks through the door each week, which has meant less attention to back-office details.

Action items to capture during the meeting:
- Update the standard grant template with the confirmed charity registration number.
- Create a one-page "Organization Info" cheat sheet for staff and volunteers who help with forms.
- Schedule a follow-up compliance review in June.

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: intake@daytonhousingnetwork.org
Cc: director@riverbendyfs.org
Date: 2025-02-06 09:18 AM
Subject: Referral & Background Info – Riverbend Youth & Family Services

Hi Carla,

Thanks again for taking the time to talk yesterday about getting Riverbend formally connected to your housing stability collaborative. As requested, I’m sending a brief overview of who we are and how we were referred, along with a recent example that illustrates why this partnership matters.

Riverbend Youth & Family Services is an after-school and family support program based at the Willow Creek Community Center on Dayton’s north side. We primarily serve youth from Riverbend Middle, North Dayton High, and nearby elementary schools, along with their caregivers.

In terms of your intake form, for the “organization information > referred by” question, please list us as having been referred by Dayton Community Impact Coalition. We’ve been attending their quarterly neighborhood safety and housing meetings since last summer, and they suggested we connect with your network because so many of our families’ crises are housing-related.

Because our purpose is to be a safety net when everything else is falling apart, we’ve informally become a place where families disclose very personal crises. At our Parent Advisory Circle in January 2024, the meeting turned into an impromptu support group when one mother shared that her partner had been arrested on New Year’s Eve for a probation violation tied to an old domestic violence charge. Overnight she was alone with two kids (one at Riverbend Middle, one at North Dayton High), no income, and an eviction notice for $1,450 in back rent on their Riverside Drive apartment.

Situations like hers are why we’re now explicitly naming housing stability and safety from domestic violence as core to our purpose, not just “extra services.”

Let me know if you need anything else.

Best,
Melissa

Melissa Anne Henderson
Program Director, Riverbend Youth & Family Services
mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org | 937-555-0142

metadata.subject: Referral & Background Info – Riverbend Youth & Family Services

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: grants@ohcommunityfund.org
Date: 2025-01-08 09:17 AM
Subject: Clarifying referral source on Riverbend YFS capacity grant

Hi Jordan,

Thanks again for walking me through the online portal yesterday. I’m working my way through the “organization information” section this morning and got stuck on the “Referred By” question.

Technically, I first heard about this capacity-building opportunity from a short blurb in the Montgomery County Nonprofit Alliance newsletter back in November, and then it came up again when I was talking with Carla from Neighborhood Bridges Collaborative at a school partners meeting. When we chatted, she said your team had encouraged smaller youth-serving agencies like ours to apply, especially those trying to stabilize after rapid enrollment growth.

Our organization was originally set up mainly to help keep a couple of after-school programs afloat when the school district cut their contracts, and we’re still figuring out a more clearly defined mission beyond “general youth and family support.” That’s part of why this grant feels like such a good fit.

Can you let me know what you’d prefer I list in the “Referred By” field—“Montgomery County Nonprofit Alliance newsletter,” “Neighborhood Bridges Collaborative,” or just “community partners”?

I’d like to be consistent with how other applicants are answering it so your team can track which outreach channels are working.

Best,
Melissa

Melissa Anne Henderson
Program Director
Riverbend Youth & Family Services
mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org | 937-555-0142

metadata.subject: Clarifying referral source on Riverbend YFS capacity grant

artifacts.content:
From: Carla Ruiz <cruiz@neighborhoodbridges.org>
To: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
Date: 2024-12-02 02:41 PM
Subject: Re: Who should I say referred us?

Hey Melissa,

Happy to hear you’re going after that operations support grant!

If the application asks “Who referred you?”, I’d just put “Neighborhood Bridges Collaborative” or my name, since I’m the one who forwarded you the link after our little huddle in the Riverbend Middle School library. The program officer told me they really want to see more grassroots youth orgs like Riverbend YFS in the mix, not just the big, polished agencies.

You could also mention that you’d been seeing it pop up in the Montgomery County Nonprofit Alliance listserv, but that our chat is what nudged you to actually start the application. That’s basically the truth, right?

Also, I know you’re worried about how the reviewers will see that boundary violation situation with the former part-time Youth Mentor, Marcus Lee, last March at your North Main Street site. Honestly, the fact that you spent around $3,200 between March and June 2024 on employment law advice and mandatory retraining shows you took it seriously once the parent reported the inappropriate late-night texts with her 15-year-old. If it comes up, frame it as strengthening your policies.

Call me later if you want to talk through any of the narrative questions.

In solidarity,
Carla

Carla Ruiz
Program Coordinator
Neighborhood Bridges Collaborative

metadata.subject: Re: Who should I say referred us?

artifacts.content:
Title: Grant application check-in – referral & narrative
Date: 2025-01-10 11:00 AM–12:00 PM
Location: Willow Creek Community Center – Melissa’s office
Attendees: Melissa Henderson; Eric (tentative – phone)

Agenda/Notes:

- Finalize answers in “Organization Information” section for the Ohio Community Fund capacity grant.
- Double-check how to phrase the “Referred By” question. Options we’ve tossed around:
  • Montgomery County Nonprofit Alliance e-newsletter
  • Neighborhood Bridges Collaborative peer referral (Carla)
  • Riverbend Middle School counselor network
- Make sure the referral story matches what I’ve mentioned in earlier emails to Jordan at the foundation.
- Re-read the draft paragraph about how Riverbend Youth & Family Services started. Right now it says we were originally set up mainly to help keep a couple of after-school programs afloat when the district cut their contracts, and that we’re still working toward a clearer mission than just “general youth and family support.” Decide if that sounds too unfocused.
- Add a short explanation of the corrective action plan after the Marcus Lee incident last March, focusing on the positive outcomes (updated policies, staff retraining) rather than just the problem.

metadata.title: Grant application check-in – referral & narrative
metadata.location: Willow Creek Community Center – Melissa’s office

artifacts.content:
From: Jordan Ellis <jellis@ohcommunityfund.org>
To: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
Date: 2025-01-07 04:06 PM
Subject: Re: Quick question about referral section

Hi Melissa,

Good to hear from you, and I’m glad you’re applying.

On the “Referred By” item, we’re not too strict. It’s mainly for our internal tracking so we know which outreach channels are actually reaching organizations like Riverbend Youth & Family Services.

If you first saw the opportunity in the Montgomery County Nonprofit Alliance newsletter and then decided to apply after talking with Carla from Neighborhood Bridges Collaborative, you can list either one. Many applicants simply put the first place they saw the announcement (e.g., “county nonprofit newsletter,” “peer organization,” or “school district partner”).

If it helps, think about what would be most accurate if we asked, “How did this come across your radar?” and answer from that perspective. You don’t need to overthink it or name every single touchpoint.

And thank you for being so candid on the application about the situation with your former Youth Mentor, Marcus Lee. It’s clear you invested significant resources—both financial and time—into legal consultation and staff retraining after the parent reported the texting issue. We view that as a sign of responsible leadership, not a red flag.

Let me know if you run into any technical snags with the portal.

Best,
Jordan

Jordan Ellis (they/them)
Program Officer, Ohio Community Fund

metadata.subject: Re: Quick question about referral section

artifacts.content:
Title: Zoom – MC Nonprofit Alliance small orgs roundtable
Date: 2024-11-19 03:30 PM–5:00 PM
Location: Zoom (link in MCNA email)
Attendees: Melissa Henderson; MC Nonprofit Alliance members

Description/Notes:

Quarterly roundtable for small-to-mid-sized nonprofits.

Goals for Melissa:
- Ask how other youth-serving agencies are handling the "Referred By" questions on various grant portals (some say to list the newsletter, others say to name the colleague who forwarded the opportunity).
- Mention that I first noticed the Ohio Community Fund capacity-building grant in the MC Nonprofit Alliance bulletin but only paid attention after Carla at Neighborhood Bridges Collaborative flagged that it was a good fit for Riverbend Youth & Family Services.
- Get examples of language others use to describe their origin story; ours is still basically, "We were set up to keep a couple of after-school programs afloat when the district cut their contracts," and we’re still trying to move beyond that vague "general youth and family support" description.
- If there’s time, ask if anyone has found low-cost HR/legal consultants who understand small youth programs. We already spent over $3,000 last spring dealing with the Marcus Lee boundary situation and mandatory retraining, and I don’t want to be caught flat-footed again.

Prep:
- Skim last MCNA newsletter for any updates on upcoming funding opportunities.
- Jot down questions about referral tracking and what funders actually do with that info.
- Test Zoom audio in office before kids arrive for after-school program.

artifacts.content:
From: intake@daytonfamilynetwork.org
To: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: 2025-01-06 10:14 AM
Subject: Referral Source Confirmation – Riverbend Youth & Family Services

Hi Melissa,

Thank you for submitting the updated partner profile for Riverbend Youth & Family Services. Before we finalize your listing in the Dayton Family Resource Directory, we need to confirm how families typically hear about your after-school and family support programs.

On the form you started last week, you checked the box for “Referred by school or community partner” and typed in **North Dayton School Partnership Council** as the primary referral source. That’s the name that will appear in the directory unless you tell us otherwise.

Many of the other neighborhood organizations list things like “self-referral,” “probation officer,” or “church outreach” instead, but it’s completely fine to highlight the North Dayton School Partnership Council if that’s where most of your referrals originate. We just want to make sure we’re using the language you prefer when we publish your profile on the public-facing site.

Could you reply by Friday to confirm that you want **North Dayton School Partnership Council** listed as the main referral source for Riverbend? If there’s a different agency or coalition you’d rather emphasize, you can send that name and we’ll update it before the January 15 directory refresh.

Thanks again for all you and your team do for youth on the north side.

Best,

Lauren Carter
Program Coordinator
Dayton Family Network
937-555-0129

artifacts.content:
Title: Meeting with United Neighborhood Referral Collaborative
Date: 2025-02-11 2:30 PM–3:30 PM
Location: Willow Creek Community Center – Conference Room B
Attendees: Melissa Henderson; James Ortiz (United Neighborhood Referral Collaborative); Tasha Green (Family Resource Navigator)

Agenda:
1. Review current intake process at Riverbend Youth & Family Services
2. Discuss how families are being referred into after-school and emergency support services
3. Decide which referral partner to list on upcoming grant applications

Notes:
- James explained that the **United Neighborhood Referral Collaborative (UNRC)** wants to streamline how families move between agencies. He suggested that Riverbend choose ONE primary referral source to highlight on shared forms so that data is consistent.
- Melissa shared that, informally, most of the new families say they heard about Riverbend from school social workers or flyers sent home from Riverbend Middle. However, several of the newer intakes have been logged in UNRC’s shared database as coming from the **UNRC central referral line**.
- Tasha recommended that, for any standardized paperwork where Riverbend is asked “Who referred the family/organization?”, they should use **United Neighborhood Referral Collaborative** as the default answer, since that’s the umbrella network connecting them to other neighborhood services.
- James will email sample language: “Referred by: United Neighborhood Referral Collaborative” for Melissa to copy into upcoming program registration and partner intake forms.

Action Items:
- Melissa: Update internal intake template to list **United Neighborhood Referral Collaborative** as the primary referral source by 2/21.
- Tasha: Train front desk volunteers on new wording.
- James: Send sample language and logo files.

metadata.title: Meeting with United Neighborhood Referral Collaborative
metadata.location: Willow Creek Community Center – Conference Room B

artifacts.content:
From: grants@ohcommunitytrust.org
To: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: 2024-12-02 8:47 AM
Subject: Clarification Needed – Referral Partner on Mini-Grant Application

Dear Melissa,

Thank you for submitting your application for the Ohio Community Trust Youth Enrichment Mini-Grant. We appreciate the work Riverbend Youth & Family Services is doing in the North Dayton corridor.

Before we can move your proposal to the review committee, we need clarification on the “Referred By / Partner Organization” section of your application. On page 3, you listed **Northside Faith & Service Alliance** as the organization that referred Riverbend to this funding opportunity.

This is not a problem on our end; we simply want to confirm that **Northside Faith & Service Alliance** is the primary community partner you want associated with this grant. Several applicants have instead listed neighborhood associations, school districts, or their own boards of directors as the referring entity, so we want to be sure we are crediting the correct partner when we publish our award announcements.

If **Northside Faith & Service Alliance** is indeed the partner that shared this opportunity with you, please reply to this email with a brief sentence confirming that. If another organization should be named as the referral source, include the full name (e.g., “Referred by: Northside Faith & Service Alliance”) so we can update your application before the December 9 deadline.

We know these details can feel tedious, but accurate referral information helps us track how grassroots programs like yours connect to larger community networks.

Sincerely,

Danielle Price
Program Officer, Ohio Community Trust
937-555-0442

grants@ohcommunitytrust.org

metadata.subject: Clarification Needed – Referral Partner on Mini-Grant Application

artifacts.content:
Title: Phone Check-In – Referral from Northside Resource Hub
Date: 2025-01-23 9:00 AM–9:30 AM
Location: Melissa’s office – Willow Creek Community Center
Attendees: Melissa Henderson; Carla Jenkins (Northside Resource Hub)

Description:
Carla is calling to follow up on the families she referred to Riverbend Youth & Family Services through the **Northside Resource Hub** intake form.

Prep Notes (Melissa):
- Carla’s email said their case management system now auto-fills “Referred to: Riverbend Youth & Family Services” whenever a family clicks the after-school or youth mentoring options.
- She also mentioned that, on their side, they log Riverbend as “Referred by **Northside Resource Hub**” in order to track which partner organizations are sending families where. She wants to make sure Riverbend is comfortable using the same wording when asked who referred them to other networks.
- I need to be honest that we’re behind on data entry; if funders look too closely at referral sources right now, they’ll see some gaps. Between food costs going up and staff hours getting cut, I’ve been staying late just to keep our attendance sheets up to date.

Call Goals:
1. Confirm that Riverbend is okay listing **Northside Resource Hub** as the referring organization on any shared intake paperwork.
2. Ask Carla if they can flag any families who might qualify for rental assistance so Tasha can follow up.
3. Clarify what language they want us to use when we report back: “Referred by: Northside Resource Hub” vs. “Partner: Northside Resource Hub.”

Reminders:
- Have the intake spreadsheet open.
- Ask Carla whether their hub can help us recruit volunteers for homework help on Thursdays.

metadata.title: Phone Check-In – Referral from Northside Resource Hub
metadata.location: Melissa’s office – Willow Creek Community Center

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: Carla Jennings <cjennings@daytoncommunityfund.org>
Date: 2025-02-18 09:42 AM
Subject: Follow-up details for Riverbend YFS capacity-building proposal

Hi Carla,

Thank you again for taking the time yesterday to talk through Riverbend Youth & Family Services’ capacity-building needs. I really appreciate how concrete your questions were about staffing, benefits, and sustainability.

Per your request, I’m sending our basic organizational contact info up front:

Riverbend Youth & Family Services
Willow Creek Community Center
2147 Willow Creek Avenue
Dayton, OH 45405
Main program line: (937) 555-2840

That number rings at our front desk from 2:00–7:30 PM on school days; outside those hours it rolls to my work cell.

You’d also asked for a brief summary of why we’re prioritizing support around staff policies. In October–November 2023 I had an emergency medical leave after being hospitalized twice at Miami Valley Hospital for complications from untreated hypertension and stress-related migraines. Because our leave policies were so informal, our small team had to scramble to cover my six-week absence as Program Director. We ended up paying about $1,800 in overtime to our Program Coordinator and even shifted several youth leadership sessions from our Willow Creek Avenue site to a reduced schedule at the downtown Dayton library just to stay afloat.

That experience really exposed how vulnerable we are when a key staff person is out, and it’s part of why this “other” support around structuring benefits and leave is so critical for us now.

If it’s helpful, I can pull together a one-page summary of our current staffing chart and draft PTO/leave ideas before your grants committee meets next Thursday.

Best,
Melissa

Melissa Anne Henderson
Program Director, Riverbend Youth & Family Services
mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org

metadata.subject: Follow-up details for Riverbend YFS capacity-building proposal

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: Front Desk <info@riverbendyfs.org>
CC: Karen Ellis <kellis@riverbendyfs.org>
Date: 2025-01-13 08:47 AM
Subject: Double-check main contact numbers before grant submission

Hi team,

I’m finalizing the United Community Fund application this morning and need to be absolutely sure we’re listing the correct organizational phone number on all external documents.

Right now my draft has the Willow Creek Community Center front desk line, (937) 555-1179, as the primary number, with my direct office line, (937) 555-6623, as the secondary contact for program referrals. Before I hit submit, can you confirm that we still want the general inquiries and new family intakes to go through the front desk instead of my line?

Part of the narrative section talks about how our organization’s purpose is deeply shaped by what families in our neighborhood are actually carrying. For example, I described how in March 2023 Karen and I started a closed trauma-support circle at the center for six teens who had either witnessed or lost a sibling or cousin to shootings along North Main Street between 2020 and 2022, including the 16-year-old whose older brother was killed outside the Speedway on Salem Avenue in October 2021. Since that’s such sensitive content, I don’t want a wrong phone number to send people to a dead line.

Can someone reply by noon so I can lock this down?

Thanks,
Melissa

metadata.subject: Double-check main contact numbers before grant submission

artifacts.content:
From: Riverbend Youth & Family Services <noreply@riverbendyfs.org>
To: All Staff <allstaff@riverbendyfs.org>
Date: 2024-11-04 03:12 PM
Subject: UPDATED Phone Tree & Emergency Contact Procedures

Hi everyone,

As enrollment has climbed this fall, we’ve had a few close calls with misdirected calls during after-school hours, so we’re cleaning up our phone tree.

Effective Monday, 11/11, please post the following by your workstation:

• Main program line (parent inquiries, attendance calls, and general questions): (937) 555-9934  
• Staff office suite (non-urgent messages, partners, and volunteers): (937) 555-4482  
• On-site evening supervisor cell (ONLY for true emergencies between 5:00–8:00 PM): (937) 555-7706

Do NOT give out personal cell numbers to youth or families; route them to the main program line first. For mandated-report situations or complex family crises, remember that Melissa may need to stay on the phone for extended periods with outside agencies. In May 2024, for instance, she spent nearly three hours on the phone with Montgomery County Children Services and the on-call social worker from East Dayton Community Health Center after a 7th grader disclosed that her stepfather had been arrested for a domestic violence incident on Riverside Drive.

Updated laminated phone-tree cards will be placed in each classroom, the gym, and the kitchen by Friday.

Thank you,
Admin Team
Riverbend Youth & Family Services

metadata.subject: UPDATED Phone Tree & Emergency Contact Procedures

From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: Dayton Community Foundation <grants@daytoncf.org>
Date: 2025-01-27 01:04 PM
Subject: Clarification on Riverbend contact info for grant agreement

Hello,

Thank you for sending over the draft grant agreement for the Riverbend Youth & Family Services After-School Resilience Initiative. I’m reviewing the contact section and wanted to correct one detail so payments and partner referrals don’t get misrouted.

The phone number listed on page 2 (937-555-4482 is actually our staff office suite line, which is not consistently staffed during school-day hours. For grant-related questions, site visits, or media inquiries, please use my direct office line: 937-555-6623. Parents and youth continue to be directed to our main program line, 937-555-9934, which routes through our intake process.

I also noticed the program description in the appendix leaves out a key piece of why our work looks the way it does. Our organization’s purpose is deeply shaped by what families in our neighborhood are carrying. In March 2023, for example, our licensed social worker, Karen Ellis, and I started a closed trauma-support circle at the Willow Creek Community Center for six teens who had either witnessed or lost a sibling or cousin to shootings along North Main Street between 2020 and 2022, including a 16-year-old from North Dayton High whose brother was killed outside the Speedway on Salem Avenue in October 2021.

Let me know if you’d like revised language for that section.

Best,
Melissa Anne Henderson  
Program Director, Riverbend Youth & Family Services  
Office: 937-555-6623

Title: Phone system check + protocol refresher
Date: 2024-12-02 02:00–03:00 PM
Location: Willow Creek Community Center – Conference Room B
Attendees: Melissa Henderson; Evening Coordinators; Front Desk Volunteers

Description:

Quarterly walkthrough of all Riverbend phone lines to ensure parents, youth, and partners can reach us without getting bounced around or hitting dead numbers.

Checklist:

1. Call each active line from a cell phone to confirm routing and voicemail:
   - (937) 555-9934 – main program line (should ring at front desk, then roll to voicemail after 6 rings).  
   - (937) 555-1179 – Willow Creek building front desk (non-program inquiries, rentals).  
   - (937) 555-4482 – staff office suite (used by tutors and part-time staff).  
   - (937) 555-7706 – evening supervisor cell for true emergencies only.

2. Review what constitutes an “emergency” vs. a situation that can wait until the next business day. Use May 2024 example when a 7th grader disclosed that her stepfather had been arrested for a domestic violence incident on Riverside Drive; Melissa spent nearly three hours on the phone with Montgomery County Children Services and the on-call social worker from East Dayton Community Health Center to make a mandated report and safety plan.

3. Update the laminated phone protocol sheets posted near every classroom phone and in the gym.

4. Decide whether to add a short recorded menu to the main program line or keep it as a direct ring-through to a live person during program hours.

metadata.title: Phone system check + protocol refresher

From: frontdesk@riverbendyfs.org
To: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org
Date: March 3, 2026 8:17 AM
Subject: Updated Main Line & Phone Tree Script

Hi Melissa,

Following up on yesterday’s staff huddle, we’ve completed the switch to the new VOIP provider. Effective immediately, the main number families should use to reach Riverbend Youth & Family Services is **(937) 555-9027**. Please stop using the old rollover line that ended in 1134 on any flyers or email signatures.

When you update your signature, comms templates, and the after-school enrollment packets, please list it exactly as:

Riverbend Youth & Family Services
Willow Creek Community Center
Dayton, OH 45405
Phone: (937) 555-9027

We want to be sure this is the number caregivers see when they’re calling about homework help, behavior concerns, or emergency food referrals. Our organizational purpose is also rooted in the realities of family instability we see every week. In May 2022, for instance, our family resource navigator, Angela Ruiz, worked intensively with a grandmother caregiver from Lincoln Elementary who had just taken emergency custody of her 9-year-old grandson after Montgomery County Children Services removed him from his mother’s apartment on Catalpa Drive for neglect related to her opioid relapse. That’s exactly the kind of situation where clear contact info really matters.

Can you confirm your staff and volunteers are using the updated number on their reminder texts to parents this week?

Thanks,
Tasha
Administrative Coordinator

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: Front Desk <info@riverbendyfs.org>
Cc: Board Program Committee <programcommittee@riverbendyfs.org>
Date: 2025-01-09 08:47 AM
Subject: Confirming main contact line for upcoming grant application

Hi all,

I’m filling out the organizational information section for the United Community Impact mini-grant and need to double-check what we’re listing as the primary **representative phone number** for Riverbend Youth & Family Services.

In the past, I’ve sometimes put the center’s main desk line, (937) 555-0198, since that’s what parents already have on the flyers. But the grant form specifically asks for “phone number for organizational representative,” which sounds more like they want a direct line for whoever is named as the contact person.

Last year, when we applied for the food security funds, I’m pretty sure we used the shared program office line, (937) 555-7730, with my name as the program director. That number rolls over to voicemail if I’m in the gym or at a school meeting, and I can usually call folks back the same day.

Can you confirm which number we’re currently using in our standard boilerplate language for proposals? I don’t want to submit something inconsistent with what’s on our letterhead and website.

Side note: while I was drafting the “community needs” section, I added a paragraph about families asking for informal financial coaching. During our August 2024 Back-to-School Resource Fair, three parents quietly pulled me aside about wage garnishments and back child support. One mom, Tasha, even showed me a letter from Montgomery County Domestic Relations Court (dated 8/5/24) saying $187 would be taken from each biweekly paycheck at the Kroger on North Gettysburg, and she was terrified she’d have to pull her son from our program.

Thanks,
Melissa

metadata.subject: Confirming main contact line for upcoming grant application

artifacts.content:
From: Denise Carter <dcarter@daytonschools.org>
To: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
Date: 2024-12-02 02:16 PM
Subject: Re: School partnership MOU – contact info section

Hi Melissa,

Thanks for sending over the draft MOU. Overall it looks solid. I just have a question about the “organization information” box on page 1, specifically the **phone number for representative**.

Right now you’ve typed in (937) 555-6682 next to your name. Is that your office line or a general Riverbend number? For our records, we usually prefer a number where our counselors can reach the program contact directly if there’s an urgent issue with a student after school.

If (937) 555-6682 is the main Willow Creek Community Center number, would you rather list your program office line instead? I still have an old form from 2022 where Riverbend used (937) 555-4309 as the contact number for the after-school program director, but I’m not sure if that’s current.

Once you confirm which number you want, I’ll have our superintendent’s office sign and we can get this in place before winter break. The section you added about supporting families with financial stressors (including wage garnishments and back child support concerns that came up at your August resource fair) was really helpful context.

Best,
Denise

Dayton Public Schools

artifacts.content:
From: info@daytoncommunityfoundation.org
To: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
Date: 2024-11-18 09:05 AM
Subject: Clarification needed – Riverbend Youth & Family Services contact details

Dear Melissa,

Thank you for submitting your Letter of Inquiry for the Dayton Community Foundation Youth Opportunity Fund. Before we can advance your proposal to the next review stage, we need to clarify a discrepancy in the “Organization Information” section.

In your uploaded LOI PDF, the “phone number for organizational representative” is listed as (937) 555-9921, while the online form you completed on our portal lists (937) 555-0198 as the representative phone number for Riverbend Youth & Family Services.

To avoid confusion during follow-up, please reply to this email confirming which number should be used as the primary representative contact for Riverbend and ensure that it connects directly (or via voicemail) to the program director or executive director. If the numbers above are outdated or general front desk lines, you may instead provide a more direct line. For example, if you have a dedicated office line (e.g., (937) 555-4309 or similar) that is better monitored, we are happy to update our records.

We also appreciate the detail in your description of family needs, including the mention of parents seeking help with wage garnishments and back child support at your August 2024 back-to-school event. This aligns with several other applications we’ve seen highlighting financial stress among working parents.

Please respond by November 25 so that your LOI can remain under consideration.

Sincerely,

Grants Administration Team
Dayton Community Foundation

metadata.subject: Clarification needed – Riverbend Youth & Family Services contact details

Title: Phone/voicemail check-in with new families

Location: Program Office – Riverbend Youth & Family Services
Attendees: Melissa Henderson

Description:

Block of time for Melissa to make welcome calls to new after-school program families and verify that they have the correct **representative phone number** for Riverbend Youth & Family Services.

Call script notes:
- Introduce self as Program Director.
- Confirm parent/guardian cell number and preferred contact method (call vs. text).
- Ask if they have the main Riverbend contact number written down correctly.

Numbers currently in circulation that parents might be using:
- (937) 555-0198 – front desk line printed on older flyers.
- (937) 555-7730 – number listed on last year’s snack program grant.
- (937) 555-9921 – shared program office line Melissa has been using on recent forms.

Goal is to gently steer families toward using the shared program office line for anything related to attendance, late pickups, behavior concerns, or family resource referrals, while still letting them know the front desk is available for general questions.

Additional reminder:
Mention during calls that Riverbend can connect families with partner agencies that offer basic financial coaching. Melissa has been hearing more stories like the mom at the August 2024 Back-to-School Resource Fair who showed her a court letter about wage garnishments taking $187 from each paycheck, and she wants parents to know they’re not alone and there are resources.

Follow-up:
- Note preferred language and best time to call in each student’s file.
- Flag any urgent family needs for the resource navigator.

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: Carla Ruiz <cruiz@daytoncf.org>
Date: February 27, 2025 9:18 AM
Subject: Updated contact info for site visits

Hi Carla,

I’m glad we were able to confirm the site visit dates for the youth mentoring grant. Our board is really excited to have you all in the building when the after-school program is in full swing.

Per your request, here’s the best contact information for the day of your visit:

– Main front desk at Willow Creek Community Center (where Riverbend leases space): (937) 555-1129  
– My direct office line: (937) 555-9034  
– Riverbend general voicemail box (after hours): (937) 555-7710

If anything comes up and you can’t reach me on the office line, you can also text my work cell at 937-555-6402. That phone is usually on me when I’m moving between the homework room and the gym.

For anything related to medical or crisis follow-up, we’ve been told to route funder questions to the hospital or school partners directly. For example, when our part-time Youth Mentor, Jasmine Carter, drove one of our 9th graders to the emergency department at Kettering Health Dayton on the evening of February 22, 2024, after he showed her a half-empty bottle of his mother’s antidepressants on FaceTime and said he was thinking about hurting himself, I spent the next morning on the phone with the hospital social worker and his mom. That situation really highlighted how fuzzy our liability and boundaries can feel.

Let me know if you need a single “official contact” listed somewhere differently for your paperwork.

Best,
Melissa

metadata.location: Melissa’s office – Willow Creek Community Center

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Henderson <mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org>
To: Sarah Klein <sklein@daytonps.org>
Date: November 14, 2024 3:56 PM
Subject: Contact number to list on Riverbend referral form

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for meeting with me and Principal Davis about tightening up the referral process from Riverbend Middle to our after-school program.

You’d asked what phone number you should list on the school’s internal referral form for the “organization representative” section. Please don’t use my personal cell; I’ve had a few situations where families have called or texted late at night about issues that really should go through crisis services or the school.

Instead, please list:

Riverbend Youth & Family Services – Program Office  
Representative phone: (937) 555-9086  
Email: mhenderson@riverbendyfs.org

During program hours (roughly 2:00–7:30 PM), someone is almost always near that office phone. If no one picks up, the voicemail forwards to my inbox.

For anything that feels urgent or safety-related, we’d prefer that school staff follow your existing crisis protocols and then loop us in the next day. We’re still working through what’s appropriate for staff to handle outside program hours—especially after the situation last winter when one of our part-time mentors drove a student to Kettering Health Dayton late in the evening and I ended up coordinating with the hospital social worker and the family the following morning.

Let me know if the district needs the number in any specific format for your data system.

Best,
Melissa

metadata.subject: Contact number to list on Riverbend referral form

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with United Way – intake form corrections
Date: December 5, 2024 11:00 AM–11:45 AM
Location: Zoom (Melissa’s office)

Attendees:
- Melissa Henderson – Riverbend Youth & Family Services
- Jordan Patel – United Way program officer

Description/Notes:
Jordan flagged a few inconsistencies on the United Way community partner intake form that need to be corrected before their database goes live in January.

Items to cover:
- Organization listing currently shows Riverbend’s old address from when the program ran out of the church basement. Update to Willow Creek Community Center address.
- The “primary organizational representative phone number” field is still populated with a temporary Google Voice number Melissa used during the early pandemic: 937-555-6120. Jordan needs a stable number that will reach the program even if staff change.
- Decide whether to use the Riverbend main office line, (937) 555-9086, or the Willow Creek Community Center front desk, (937) 555-1100, as the official public contact. Jordan prefers one single number.
- Confirm that crisis/after-hours calls should NOT be routed to individual staff. Melissa will explain the recent incident in which a part-time Youth Mentor transported a youth to Kettering Health Dayton after hours, and how that raised questions about boundaries and liability.
- Verify spelling of Melissa’s title (Program Director) and add a backup contact person in case she’s out.

Prep: Melissa to pull up a copy of the original intake form, plus any recent flyers to ensure consistency in how Riverbend’s contact info is listed.

metadata.title: Call with United Way – intake form corrections
